symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic high-volume watery non-bloody diarrhoea (2 months)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secretory, high-volume, non-bloody watery diarrhoea is the hallmark clinical pattern of microscopic colitis.
A 2-month chronic timeline argues strongly against an acute self-limiting infection.
Nocturnal diarrhoea indicates an organic (secretory) rather than functional process.
IBS characteristically does NOT wake patients at night; nocturnal symptoms are an alarm feature pointing away from a functional diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of blood, mucus or pus in the stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Microscopic colitis produces watery, non-bloody stool despite mucosal inflammation.
Bloody/mucoid diarrhoea is typical of active IBD; its absence lowers but does not exclude IBD.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild diffuse crampy abdominal discomfort without severe or localised pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of severe, sharp or localised pain argues against perforation, obstruction or an acute surgical process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional 3 kg weight loss over 2 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight loss with a change in bowel habit in an older adult is an alarm feature mandating exclusion of malignancy.
Ongoing fluid and nutrient loss from chronic diarrhoea can cause modest weight loss.
NSAIDs are a well-recognised precipitant of microscopic colitis; identifying and stopping the trigger is central to management.
Microscopic colitis is strongly associated with autoimmune disease, including autoimmune thyroiditis.
Autoimmune thyroid disease increases coeliac risk, warranting serological screening.
Postural lightheadedness is a patient-reportable proxy for volume depletion; the objective orthostatic BP drop is clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic blood-pressure drop on standing (mild volume depletion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured postural blood-pressure drop confirms volume depletion — a sealed measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with mild diffuse tenderness, no guarding/rigidity/rebound, normal bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign abdominal examination excludes peritonism/obstruction — but it is clinician-performed and unobtainable via telehealth, which is precisely why an in-person review is required.

investigation_details.expected_result: May be normal or mildly raised in microscopic colitis; markedly raised suggests IBD
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to help discriminate microscopic colitis from inflammatory bowel disease

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Calprotectin helps stratify the need for and urgency of endoscopic assessment.

investigation_details.expected_result: Macroscopically normal mucosa; histology shows intraepithelial lymphocytosis and/or a thickened subepithelial collagen band
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis cannot be confirmed and colorectal neoplasia/IBD cannot be excluded

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology on biopsy is the only definitive confirmation of microscopic colitis — impossible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stool microscopy/culture and C. difficile toxin

investigation_details.expected_result: No pathogen / negative toxin expected, excluding chronic infection
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Chronic infective diarrhoea (e.g. Giardia, C. difficile) not excluded

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stool studies are required to exclude an infective cause of chronic diarrhoea.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic nocturnal watery non-bloody diarrhoea in an older female with chronic NSAID use and autoimmune disease is the classic microscopic-colitis constellation — but remains presumptive until histology.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: organic disease requiring in-person assessment and endoscopy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Weight loss plus volume depletion in an older adult living alone mandates urgent in-person assessment and colonoscopy — the reason the correct tier is a T3 uplift rather than remote reassurance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (renal function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess dehydration severity and exclude acute kidney injury from ongoing fluid loss.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool MCS, C. difficile toxin and faecal calprotectin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude chronic infection and help discriminate microscopic colitis from IBD.
Autoimmune thyroid disease raises coeliac risk; screen given chronic diarrhoea.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Colonoscopy with biopsy (via urgent gastroenterology referral)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive histological diagnosis and exclusion of colorectal neoplasia and IBD in an older adult with weight loss.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad stool pathogen PCR panel before any assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-ordering an exhaustive panel remotely is unnecessary; targeted stool studies are arranged through the in-person review.
